articles.title: BATTLE-TESTED AI PROMPTS
articles.summary: Spending your mornings tweaking AI tools to get the results your brand needs? Looking for something beyond "caption this image" prompts?

articles.title: YET ANOTHER GLOBAL STUDY SAYS INSTAGRAM AND TIKTOK ARE BAD FOR YOUR MENTAL HEALTH
articles.summary: A new global study tied to the World Happiness Report 2026 finds that algorithm-driven platforms are more strongly associated with poorer mental health than communication-focused apps like WhatsApp and Facebook. The distinction is how people use social media rather than how much, with passive scrolling and curated content linked to lower life satisfaction, while direct interaction shows neutral or positive effects. The impact is strongest among younger users, particularly teenage girls, where heavy daily use correlated with significantly lower happiness levels.

articles.title: WHAT KEEPS VIEWERS WATCHING FACEBOOK REELS?
articles.summary: Facebook Reels performance is driven by early hooks and format, not trends. Videos with speech in the first 3 seconds see +24.7% higher 10-second retention and +5.6% more engagement than music-only content. Vertical videos boost reach by +20.9% and 30-second retention by +38.5%. Showing a person early increases short-term retention by +10.1%. For very short clips, seamless loops lift replays by +18.7% and reach by +23.6%. Prioritize voice, human presence, and vertical format to keep viewers watching.

articles.title: RETENTION TEAR SHEET
articles.summary: Start small by focusing on one high-dropoff point, like onboarding or re-engagement, and build a targeted program around it. Work closely with customer success to understand real user pain points, and track behavior that signals progress like activation or feature use, not just email metrics. Repurpose existing content instead of adding tools, and formally define lifecycle as a function so it can be owned and measured. Tie your first program directly to a revenue metric like activation, churn, or expansion to prove impact before scaling.

articles.title: THREAD WALLETS MARKETING BREAKDOWN
articles.summary: Thread Wallets grew by turning a basic product into a personality-driven accessory and validating demand before scaling. It proved demand early through Kickstarter, where its second campaign raised $35,000 against a $5,000 goal. The company scaled fast by using micro influencers, campus distribution, and Facebook Marketplace ads, which drove a 41% increase in return on ad spend. It focused on a clear audience, which ended up being about 90% female, and used customer data to guide decisions and avoid wasted effort.

articles.title: ALREADY RUNNING FB ADS? ADD TLDR TO YOUR MEDIA MIX.
articles.summary: TLDR reaches the technical decision makers who actually convert. MLOps Community found Meta drove 2x more registrations, but fewer

articles.title: actual attendees than TLDR. See how TLDR compares to Meta.
SYNTHETIC USERS FOR PRODUCT DISCOVERY: WHAT PEOPLE MISS (WEBINAR)
articles.summary: Most attempts at synthetic user research produce plausible-sounding nonsense due to poor setups or wrong use cases. Caitlin Sullivan, former Head of UXR at Spotify, will talk about how to use synthetic users in research at this event, which will take place on March 26 at 9:30 AM ET. The session will cover what you need to know before you waste time on bad outputs.

articles.title: HOW TO BUILD YOUR MARKETING STRATEGY IN CLAUDE
articles.summary: Most marketers get weak results from AI because their strategy is unclear or disconnected from daily work. This article shows how to fix this by building a central /marketing-strategy skill in Claude that acts as a living strategy document used in every task. It outlines 7 exercises, like ICP prioritization, positioning, and revenue levers, to create a structured and usable strategy. Teams should use this skill daily for briefs, content reviews, and prioritization so work aligns with core goals instead of random execution. Storing and updating the skill in GitHub keeps it consistent across the team and ensures strategy evolves with the business.

articles.title: THE FUTURE OF CONTENT BELONGS TO THE TASTEMAKERS
THE INTERNET RUINED CUSTOMER SERVICE. AI COULD SAVE IT
articles.summary: The internet scaled commerce but weakened customer service, turning people into tickets. Concierge-level care stayed limited to luxury brands because it's expensive. AI changes that by making always-on attention cheap and scalable. Companies can now offer high-touch service to everyone. Customer service shifts from a cost center to a driver of loyalty and revenue.

articles.title: WALMART SAYS CHATGPT CHECKOUT CONVERTED 3X WORSE THAN ITS OWN WEBSITE
articles.summary: Walmart saw in-chat purchases convert at about one-third the rate of its website.

articles.title: GOOGLE SEARCH TEST REPLACES HEADLINES AND WEBSITE TITLES WITH AI
articles.summary: Google is testing AI-rewritten search headlines, raising concerns about publisher impact.

articles.title: EVERYONE HATES ADS ON SOCIAL MEDIA. OR DO THEY?
articles.summary: Social media ads aren't as disruptive or as disliked as people think.
